Name: Bitcoin
Description: Created in January 2009, Bitcoin is the first decentralized cryptocurrency. Bitcoin serves as a completely digital payment system without any central authority taking part in the process. Bitcoin uses cryptography to keep itself secure, and Bitcoin transactions can be transparently viewed on the blockchain. In addition, the total supply limit of Bitcoin will stop at 21 million coins.

Name: Ethereum
Description: Ethereum is a smart contract platform powered by its native currency: ETH, also called Ether or Ethereum. Because Ethereum allows decentralized applications (DApps) to be built on its blockchain, its network empowers from decentralized finance (DeFi) applications, non-fungible tokens (NFTs) to blockchain games.

Name: Build 'N Build
Description: Initially created as an ERC20 token on the Ethereum network, BNB is now a native token gas token that powers the BNB chain. BNB can also be used as transaction fees on the Binance Exchange, to trade with other cryptocurrencies, to book travel arrangements, buying virtual gifts or to be used as collaterals for loans on numerous platforms. They recently rebranded the chain name to "Build 'N Build."

Name: XRP
Description: XRP is a digital asset which bridges two different currencies quickly and efficiently. XRP is the native currency of XRP Ledger, a blockchain-based, open-sourced, distributed protocol that has a transaction speed of 3-5 seconds. The company behind XRP is known as Ripple, who claims to offer “secure, instantly and nearly free global financial transactions of any size with no chargebacks".

Name: Cardano
Description: Cardano is a smart contract platform whose foundation focuses on a science-based approach. Their papers are peer-reviewed by experts internationally to validate their method. Cardano, moreover, uses the Ouroboros POS system which divides time into epochs, thus increasing scalability. In addition, the Cardano network divides itself into subnetworks to handle more transactions. It is currently deploying its first smart contracts to compete with other layer 1 protocols.

Name: Solana
Description: Solana is a blockchain created to host decentralized, scalable applications. The strength of Solana is that it can process many more transactions per seconds than Ethereum and it also charges lower transaction fees. Additionally, Solana utilizes both proof-of-stake (PoS) and proof-of-work (PoW) models.

Name: Avalanche
Description: Avalanche is an open-sourced, smart contract blockchain that focuses on transaction speed, eco-friendliness and scalability. Due to its unique structure enabling subnets for each protocl on the chain, Avalanche is by far one of the fastest layer one chains in the crypto universe.

Name: Terra
Description: Terra LUNA is the most successful chain which powers the algorithmic stablecoin known as UST. LUNA itself is the other native token of the open-sourced blockchain known as Terra. LUNA is a cryptoasset with many functions: It's primary use besides being tradeable is it can be used as collateral for the Terra economy or used for governance and mining. Unlike other layer one protocols, Terra LUNA enables transactions to be paid in multiple coins (a unique feature other chains have not followed upon).

Name: Polkadot
Description: Polkadot is a network with multiple blockchains where users can transfer every type of data and asset—including tokens, DApps…—from one blockchain to another. Spreading transactions across many parallel blockchain also improves scalability.

Name: Dogecoin
Description: Originally created as a joke, Dogecoin works as a decentralized, peer-to-peer digital currency. It is considered the first ‘meme coin’ and has gathered for itself a large community of fans and investors. Dogecoin’s success is in great parts thanks to the endorsement of Reddit and Elon Musk.

Name: Shiba Inu
Description: Shiba Inu is an Ethereum-based token that is referred to by some as the ‘Dogecoin killer’. The token is “an experiment in decentralized spontaneous community building”, which means the project started from zero without a preassembled team or direction, and it’s 100% up to the community to build something out of it.

Name: Polygon
Description: Polygon is a layer 2 scaling solution working on top of the Ethereum blockchain. What Polygon brings to the table is faster and lower-cost transactions than Ethereum, and these transactions are also irreversible on the Ethereum mainchain.

Name: Cronos
Description: Cronos is the utility token of Cronos EVM Chain and Crypto.org Chain. The Cronos EVM Chain supports DeFi apps and allows users to port smart contracts and DApps from Ethereum and all EVM compatible chains. Crypto.com itself offers many services like wallet and card app, MCO Visa card,... with the purpose of accelerating the transition of the tradfi economy  into the native cryptocurrency landscape.

Name: Litecoin
Description: Litecoin is a decentralized internet currency founded 2 years after Bitcoin by former Google engineer Charlie Lee. Litecoin is used to transfer payment around the world with near-zero cost and fast transaction speed. Athought it has lagged in development of other chains, it recently deployed an upgrade with the privacy oriented Mimble Wimble which gives it equal footing with other chains dedicated to anonymous transactions.

Name: Cosmos
Description: Cosmos is dubbed an “Internet of blockchains” by its founding team. It aims to use open-source tools to streamline transactions between different crypto networks. Cosmos focuses on customizability and interoperability with a goal to create an ecosystem of decentralized networks. Cosmos’s own PoS blockchain is called Cosmos Hub and is powered by ATOM. Many other chains have used the Cosmos SDK to buid out their own networks and is considered a layer zero chain in many theoretical circles.

Name: Chainlink
Description: Chainlink acting as a price oracle provides real-world data to smart contracts. Since most smart contracts can’t execute their terms without relying on external data sources, Chainlink incentivizes data providers to act as a bridge between the two. There are reputation scores and rewards assigned to each provider to further encourage them to provide accurate and useful data.

Name: Near
Description: NEAR protocol is a proof-of-stake (PoS) layer 1 blockchain that utilizes a sharding solution called ‘Nightshade’. This solution only lets each node to store a small subset of the platform’s data, which reduces potential points of failure security-wise. NEAR protocol also includes ‘Rainbow Bridge’, an application that lets users easily transfer Ethereum tokens to NEAR and vice versa.

Name: Tron
Description: The Tron blockchain has 3 layers to its architecture: the Core layer sends coded instructions to the Tron Virtual Machine; the Application layer lets developers create wallets and applications powered by TRX; and the Storage layer stores records of the blockchain’s history and status of smart contracts.

Name: FTX Token
Description: FTT Token is the native token of the FTX exchange, which is a centralized cryptocurrency exchange focused on derivatives, volatility products and leveraged products. FTX can be used as collateral, to get discounts on trading fees and more.

Name: Algorand
Description: Algorand aims to expand the applications of cryptocurrencies by speeding up transactions and accelerating the finality of those transactions. ALGO holders can receive more ALGO with each new block created. Algorand also empowers smart contracts and uses Proof of Stake to secure its network.

Name: Fantom
Description: FTM is a layer 1 decentralized smart contract platform aimed at greatly reducing transaction speed and scalability of blockchain technology while also maintaining high security. Ultilizing DAG technology, FTM reduces the time needed for transaction, ensures exchanges occur instantly, and handles thousands of transactions every second.

Name: Stella
Description: Stellar is an open-source, decentralized platform for fiat money that allows easy transactions between any pair of currencies. Created In 2014 by Jed McCaleb. Stellar is designed to be able to work with all of the world's financial systems. It also aims to vastly improve transaction speed and reduce the cost of transactions while maintaining high security. XLM is a decentralized fork of the XRP blockchain.

Name: Uniswap
Description: Uniswap is one of the first and largest decentralized exchanges that is hosted on the Ethereum blockchain. Uniswap pioneered the Automated Market Maker system, which uses a liquidity pool instead of cryptocurrency exchanges run by other centralized companies. Using the liquidity pool, Uniswap enables peer-to-peer exchanges to happen effortlessly, allowing anyone, from anywhere, to trade.

Name: Hedera
Description: Hedera is a decentralized public network where enterprises, developers, or consumers can build their applications with speed, fairness, and security. Hedera uses a "gossip about gossip" protocol where the individual nodes on the network "gossip" about transactions to create directed acyclic graphs that time-sequence transactions, which avoids bundling data into blocks or using miners to validate transactions.

Name: Ethereum Classic
Description: Ethereum is a decentralized, open-sourced platform that utilizes smart contracts which ensures speed, transparency, and security. After the DAO incident in 2016, Ethereum split into Ethereum and Ethereum Classic. The Classic version maintains unchanged history and is secured from external attacks on transactions.

Name: Internet Computer
Description: Internet Computer is a public blockchain network that utilizes smart contracts. Initially released in 2019 by Dfinity, Internet Computer is a  “general-purpose” blockchain that provides a public platform for hosting tokens and decentralized applications.Their ultimate goal is to create a decentralized internet to power the worlds decentralized apps (dApps).

Name: Decentraland
Description: Decentraland is a virtual-based platform where players can create and monetize virtual plots of land as NFT through the use of the MANA cryptocurrency. Decentraland aims at creating an immersive virtual world in the metaverse where users can socialize, interact, learn and play. Decentraland is a decentralized world where users can explore land owned by other users or trade virtual assets such as NFTs.

Name: Axie Infinity
Description: Axie Infinity tokens are ERC-20 tokens used in the Axie Infinity Universe. A minimum of 3 Axie Infinity tokens is required to begin playing and earning from the Axie Infinity Universe. AXS is mainly used for in-game purchases such as other users' "Axies" (creatures that can be collected, bred, and battled), or plots of lands and other items in the form of NFTs.

Name: Vechain
Description: VeChain is a public blockchain platform that aims at improving supply chain management and business procedures. Vechain's main token is denoted as VET, an ERC-20 on the Ethereum blockchain. VeChain aims at connecting blockchain technology to the world by providing infrastructures and IOT intergration.

Name: Osmosis
Description: Osmosis is a decentralized, open-source, peer-to-peer blockchain primarily used as an AMM. Osmosis adopts the Inter-Blockchain-Communication (IBC) that allows fast and cost-effective connection to the Osmosis SDK network. By using Osmosis, you can create liquidity and trade IBC-enabled tokens.

Name: Filecoin
Description: Filecoin is a public cryptocurrency and digital payment system intended to be a blockchain-based method of co-operating digital storage and retrieval. Filecoin is an open protocol and backed by a blockchain that records commitments made by the network's participants, with transactions recorded in FIL, the blockchain's native currency.

Name: Monero
Description: Monero is a decentralized cryptocurrency. Unlike other cryptocurrencies, Monero boasts privacy features. Monero's blockchain cannot track or trace transactions. To create new coins and secure transactions, Monero uses a proof of work consensus algorithm.

Name: Elrond
Description: A distributed transactional computation protocol built on the Elrond concept, Elrond uses a secure Proof of Stake consensus method and sharded state architecture. Elrond runs on ordinary computers, contrary to most blockchain networks, which use custom hardware and high energy consumption. Using the sharding method, Elrond's performance will scale with the number of computers joining the network.

Name: Klaytn
Description: Klaytn is a public blockchain platform built by Kakao, South Korea's most popular mobile platform. Using an efficient 'hybrid' design, it combines the advantages of public blockchain ( decentralized data & control, distributed governance), and private blockchains (decentralized control, distributed governance).

Name: Theta Network
Description: Theta Network is a decentralized video delivery network that integrates blockchain technology as well as users' computer power to improve video quality. Theta aims at bolstering video quality and speed of video delivery in countries where the infrastructure required is not up to the task. In exchange for users' contributions to the distribution network, they will be rewarded with THETA tokens.

Name: Tezos
Description: Tezos is a decentralized, open-source blockchain that can deploy smart contracts and allow peer-to-peer transactions. Tezos utilizes liquidity proof-of-stake, which allows it to adjust itself automatically when its upgrade proposal gets enough votes from the community.

Name: Magic Internet Money
Description: Abracadabra decentralized money lending platform's Magic Internet Money is an ERC - 20 token and is soft-pegged to the US Dollar. MIM is backed by interest-bearing tokens and can be traded with other stable coins.

Name: OKB
Description: OKB is a token introduced by OK Blockchain Foundation, with a purpose to build an OKEx ecosystem to accelerate the development of blockchain technology and digital asset industry. The acceleration is achieved by using OKB to connect users, investors and digital assets projects.

Name: PancakeSwap
Description: CAKE token is issued by PancakeSwap, a decentralized exchange specializing in swapping BEP-20 tokens, which are tokens built on the Binance Smart Chain that don’t have their own blockchain. PancakeSwap has one of the largest daily user counts of any dApp and it can also connect to many different wallets. CAKE can be used for staking with an impressively stable but high APY.

Name: Flow
Description: After their NFT token launch rendering the Ethereum blockchain almost unusable due to traffic jam, the creators behind CryptoKitties created a blockchain they hope would solve this problem. The Flow playground, therefore, is a place for developers to create and trade NFTs; but it is a blockchain with a multi-node architecture (collection nodes, consensus nodes, execution nodes and verification nodes), each node handling separate responsibilities in order to maximize speed.

Name: Gala
Description: In a market saturated with many opportunist cash-grabs, Gala Games aims to be a blockchain games studio that not only creates games that are enjoyable to play but players can also take ownership of their in-game items.

Name: Aave
Description: Aave is a DeFi project working as a lending protocol that users can lend, borrow and earn interest on 17 different types of crypto assets. No middleman involved, Aave runs on a system of smart contracts, using collaterals as security for the loans. In addition, Aave allows for special features such as flash loans.

Name: Maker
Description: Maker is a protocol which maintains DAI, a stablecoin designed to track the price of the US dollars, using other crypto assets. MKR is the governance token of the Maker protocol so MKR holders can vote in the MakerDAO to make changes to the protocol, such as which assets can be locked in the protocol and how much would they sell for in liquidation.

Name: Theta Fuel
Description: Theta Fuel (TFUEL) is one of the 2 native tokens running on the Theta blockchain, the other being THETA. While THETA is the governance token, TFUEL is used as a utility token and to execute transactions. TFUEL is the gas coin that powers all functions of the Theta blockchain.

Name: Bitcoin SV
Description: BSV is the native cryptocurrency of the blockchain called Bitcoin Satoshi’s Vision, which is a hard fork version of the Bitcoin Cash blockchain. Bitcoin Cash itself is another hard fork of the original Bitcoin blockchain. Bitcoin SV aims to “restore the original Satoshi protocol, keep it stable and enable it to massively scale.”

Name: Quant
Description: QNT is used to power Quant Overledger Network which aims to bridge the gap between different blockchain networks, especially public blockchains and private networks. Developers can use Quant to build decentralized cross-chain applications (MdApps or MApps).

Name: Huobi Token
Description: A native token of the Huobi Global exchange, Huobi Token (hT) is a crypto asset built on the Ethereum blockchain and is ERC-20 compliant. HT can be used to reduce fees for trading operations on the exchange, as well as getting a monthly VIP status, receive crypto rewards for seasonal buybacks and vote on the exchange governance.

Name: Arweave
Description: Arweave specializes in storing data, working as a decentralized storage network and describing itself as “a collectively owned hard drive that never forgets”. Arweave hosts a permanent and decentralized website containing community-driven applications and platforms, using AR to pay miners to maintain the network.

Name: Zcash
Description: In short, Zcash is a Bitcoin fork that prioritizes privacy. Transactions on Zcash are designed to be completely anonymous by using the Zerocash protocol to hide the sender, recipient and amount of the transactions. However, users can still choose the transparent transaction option: this is mainly for accommodating wallets or exchanges that demand transparency.

Name: Neo
Description: Neo is a platform that enables developers to design dapps that aim to mimic real world products and services. Neo uses NeoContracts instead of normal smart contracts, which lets users build applications with already pre-established languages like C# and Java. Moreover, NEO owners can vote to help operate the Neo network.

Name: KuCoin Token
Description: KuCoin is a cryptocurrency exchange, allowing users to buy, sell and store a wide variety of crypto assets. KCS, the native token of the exchange, can be used to reduce fees and earn rewards. You can create and use a KuCoin account with just an email address but to access the advanced features, the account needs to be verified. Some of the features in KuCoin are trading bots, leverage, margin…

Name: eCash
Description: eCash used to be known as Bitcoin Cash ABC (BCHA): a fork of Bitcoin (BTC) and Bitcoin Cash (BCH). eCash aims to be a virtual currency that will be actually used to pay for goods and services. Since its rebrand, eCash has introduced “bits” as its base unit, so you would send 10 bits with eCash compared to 0.00001000 BTC.

Name: Humans.ai
Description: Humans.ai aims to create an all-in-one platform for ethical AI-based creations and governance. The initial focus for Humans.ai is synthetic media, and using its token-based ownership and accountability system, the platform ensures that the AIs are uncorrupted long-term.

Name: THORchain
Description: THORchain is a decentralized liquidity protocol, which means it helps people transfer their crypto assets across a variety of networks without losing full custody of the assets. Users can utilize THORchain to swap assets with comfort in a permissionless setting. RUNE is the native token of the platform, used for governance, security and as the base token in the THORchain ecosystem.

Name: Enjin Coin
Description: Enjin Coin is an ERC-20 token built on the Ethereum network which fuels the Enjin ecosystem. It’s one of the largest gaming community platforms online with over 250,000 gaming communities and more than 18 million registered gamers. Enjin brings its value to the community with its seamless creation and management of virtual goods by directly backing the value of blockchain assets.

Name: NEXO
Description: Nexo is a lending platform for cryptocurrencies powered and backed by Credissimo - a leading FinTech Group established in 2007. The platform currently supports over 40 different currencies and has been operating since 2018.

Name: Amp
Description: AMP provides straightforward but versatile means of verifiable collateralization, through a system of collateral partitions and collateral managers. A partition can serve as insurance for essentially anything of value. These can range from accounts, applications, to even transactions. Assets are locked, released, or redirected as necessary to support value transfer activities.

Name: Celcious Network
Description: CEL is an ERC-20 token and the native currency of the Celsius Network. A centralized network that allows people to lend and borrow cryptocurrencies using a peer-to-peer lending platform. You’ll be able to earn interest on a loan or take a loan yourself, all in your desired currency.

Name: Radix
Description: Radix is the first decentralized layer one protocol specifically built to serve and improve DeFi. It’s suitable for thousands of projects to build, scale and deploy with close to unlimited financial applications. Radix has developed its own programming language called Scrypto, designed to facilitate the creation of decentralized financial applications (dApps).

Name: Celo
Description: The CELO cryptocurrency serves both as a utility token and as a governance token for the blockchain platform Celo. Powered by Proof-of-Stake (PoS) protocol, the blockchain provides users the ability to participate and earn rewards on the platform while supporting stablecoins and tokenized assets with an algorithmic reserve mechanism.

Name: Kusama
Description: Kusama is an early and highly experimental version of Polkadot that represents real-world economic conditions. Kusama is considered the real-money testnest of Polkadot which is not economically centralized and will continue to exist as long as it is maintained by the community. The team aims to cater to innovative early-stage development and deployment of new applications.

Name: Stacks
Description: Stacks is a decentralized computing network and app ecosystem that uses STX as its native currency. It’s used to fuel smart contracts for Bitcoin and rewards miners on the open Stacks network, by allowing holders to earn BTC with stacking. Applications built on Stacks inherit all the benefits of Bitcoin. In this way, decentralized apps can exist as a kind of layer two of BTC.

Name: Kadena
Description: KDA is a cryptocurrency used to pay for computational work in the Kadena public chain. In the same way that Ethereum miners receive ETH for mining blocks, Kadena miners are paid in KDA for mining blocks. It’s one of the world’s first truly scalable blockchains that are ready for applications. Several major companies have already implemented this enterprise software, including those in healthcare, finance, and insurance and carbon credit exchanges.

Name: Dash
Description: Dash is a fork of Bitcoin established in 2014 and is an acronym for “digital cash”. It is one of the oldest and longest-running decentralized autonomous organizations (DAO). Its focus is on transactions speed and privacy. With Dash, you can remain anonymous while making transactions, just like with cash. Payments are easy to make, instant, secure, and essentially free.

Name: Chiliz
Description: Chiliz is a fintech platform that aims to tokenize the sports and entertainment sectors by creating a fan engagement platform. The project essentially gives eSports and sports fans the ability to crowd-manage their favorite leagues, games, and teams. Many Premier League Teams has already issued tokens on the Chiliz network.

Name: Frax Share
Description: Frax Share is the governance token for the Frax protocol, which uses an algorithmic stablecoin system to provide decentralized and scalable money. It is designed to be volatile and to hold the rights to governance as well as all excess collateral. Furthermore, it incorporates the same ethos of Bitcoin in designing trustless money based on a highly reduced governance structure.

Name: Anchor Protocol
Description: ANC is Anchor Protocol's governance token, built and backed by Terraform Labs. It is a decentralized lending protocol on the Tera cryptocurrency blockchain. Using the protocol, a portion of Anchor's yield can be captured, so its value increases linearly with Anchor's assets. Powered by Proof of Stake returns across multiple blockchains, Anchor's yield is stable and attractive.

Name: Basic Attention Token
Description: Basic Attention Token (BAT) is an Ethereum based token specifically designed to be used with the Brave browser in order to facilitate transactions. Brave is a privacy-first browser that features built-in ad blockers among other privacy-oriented features. It provides a means of accounting among advertisers, publishers, and users of a new blockchain-based digital advertising and service platform.

Name: GateToken
Description: GateToken (GT) is the official exchange token of the Gate.io exchange and the native token of Gatechain’s public network. The network is dedicated to on-chain safety with customizable time-delay recovery features. These allow for the recovery of blockchain assets even after the compromisation of private keys.

Name: BitDAO
Description: BitDAO is one of the largest Decentralized Autonomous Organizations (DAO) in the world. BIT is the governance token for the BitDAO ecosystem, which gives users the ability to propose and vote. The project's goal is to create a decentralized tokenized economy that is accessible to everyone. By developing BitDAO DeFi products and supporting DeFi partners and projects, BitDAO will allocate funds for the growth of the DeFi ecosystem.

Name: Loopring
Description: Loopring (LRC) is a smart contract protocol built on the Ethereum blockchain. It is designed for building decentralized crypto exchanges. This is accomplished through an automated execution system that trades across various crypto-token exchanges, protecting its users from counterparty risk and reducing the trading cost.

Name: Synthethix Network Token
Description: The SNX protocol provides an on-chain access to a wide variety of both crypto and non-crypto assets. It is an Ethereum blockchain-based DeFi protocol that allows access to highly liquid financial assets (synths). Rather than holding the underlying asset directly, synths track and provide their returns. By introducing non-blockchain assets, the platform aims to broaden the cryptocurrency space and provide access to a more robust financial market.

Name: Convex Finance
Description: Convex’s goal is to simplify Curve’s staking with the help of its native fee-earning token - CVX. Liquidity providers on Curve can earn fees and boost CRV without locking their own.

Name: NEM
Description: XEM is the native cryptocurrency of NEM's NIS1 public blockchain platform, an ecosystem of platforms that use blockchain and cryptography to provide solutions to businesses and individuals. In contrast to Proof-of-Work (PoW) and Proof-of-Stake (PoS), NIS1 uses its own algorithm, proof-of-importance (PoI). In short, PoIit ensures that transaction data on the network is recorded and processed in a timely and honest manner.

Name: Curve DAO Token
Description: CRV is the native token of Curve which is a decentralized exchange for stablecoins. Curve is moreover a decentralized autonomous organization (DAO) that uses the Ethereum-based creation tool, Aragon. Through the tool, multiple smart contracts can connect to users' deposited liquidity.

Name: Oasis Network
Description: OASIS offers a scalable and privacy-centric layer-1 blockchain network with low gas fees and high throughput. By combining confidential computing with blockchain, a new paradigm called tokenized data is created. Users can earn rewards by staking their data with applications that wish to analyze it or control how sensitive information is used.

Name: Secret
Description: SCRT is the native coin of the Secret Network, which is used to pay fees and transfers. It is a privacy-centered blockchain built on the Cosmos (ATOM) ecosystem, featuring smart contracts known as Secret Contracts. These contracts allow the transmission of encrypted inputs into encrypted outputs without the actual exposing data.

Name: Link
Description: Link (LN) is a new form of investment asset with scarcity and a project launched by LINE with the goal to create a token economy and blockchain platform for mainstream users. LN is distributed to contributors in the LINE token economy who use and expand LINE’s services.

Name: Mina Protocol
Description: Mina is acknowledged as the world's lightest blockchain because of its ability to maintain a constant size despite growth in usage. Using this protocol, DApps can run more efficiently due to its minimal "succinct blockchain" built to reduce computational requirements. In comparison to Bitcoin's 300 GB blockchain, the entire Mina network takes up only 22 KB.

Name: Decred
Description: DCR is a blockchain-based cryptocurrency established in 2016. The protocol and currency place a strong emphasis on sustainability, transparent governance, and community input. The foundations of Decred are heavily influenced by Bitcoin and follow many of its core principles.

Name: Bitkub Coin
Description: KUB is the native utility token of the Bitkub Chain, which powers, operates, and governs its ecosystem. In addition to transferring KUB from one to another, it also enables you to pay transaction fees, and redeem trading fee credits through the Bitkub Exchange. Currently, Bitkub Chain has over 4 million transactions and more than 1.4 millíon wallets registered.

Name: Holo
Description: Holochain is used to create decentralized applications to be hosted on Holo, a peer-to-peer distributed platform. This is a framework for developing DApps without the need for blockchain technology. Through Holo, Holochain-based apps can be developed and made accessible to a broader community. DApps are easily accessible through the Holo ecosystem as they are hosted on the internet by Holo network members.

Name: Compound
Description: COMP is an ERC-20 governance token of the Compound Protocol. Changes to the protocol are discussed, proposed, and voted on by COMP token holders and their delegates.

Name: yearn.finance
Description: Yearn.finance is an Ethereum-based token that governs the decentralized finance (DeFi) yearn.finance platform. It simplifies DeFi for investors who are not technically minded and lets them optimize their earnings through lending of crypto assets.

Name: SafeMoon
Description: SafeMoon is community-driven decentralized finance (DeFi) token on the Binance Smar Chain (BSC). The protocol features three main functions; reflection, liquidity pool acquisition, and manual burn with all of them taking place during each trade. In each transaction, the token charges 10% of the transaction fee, of which 5% goes to token holders and 5% goes to the Binance Coin (BNB), which is controlled by the coin's authors.

Name: Sushi
Description: SushiSwap (SUSHI) is an automated market maker (AMM). A decentralized exchange that provides markets for any given pair of tokens. SushiSwap's main objective is to diversify the AMM market and also provide its users with features not present in past AMMs, such as Uniswap.

Name: 1inch
Description: 1INCH is the native token of the decentralized exchange 1Inch. The Ethereum-based token is used to vote on protocol parameters under the decentralized autonomous organization (DAO) model. Through the platform, users can discover and create the most efficient ways to swap across decentralized exchanges, bringing the best prices to the market.

Name: Ankr
Description: Ankr is a decentralized blockchain infrastructure provider that has more than 50 Proof-of-Stake networks operating around the world. The network's infrastructure drives cryptocurrency-economy growth while powering a full set of multi-chain tools for Web3 users.

Name: Render Token
Description: RenderToken (RNDR) deploys a distributed GPU rendering network that is built on the Ethereum blockchain. It connects artists and studios with mining partners who are willing to share their GPU compute power. These node operators are in return rewarded with the RNDR token which can be bought and sold on exchanges.

Name: Zilliqa
Description: Zilliqa is an open-source, permission-free blockchain that can handle thousands of transactions per second. As a second-layer scaling solution, it intends to address the problem of blockchain scalability and speed. The platform hosts many decentralized applications on the platform, as well as staking and yield farming.

Name: Gnosis
Description: GNO is the native Ethereum-based token of the Gnosis Chain. The network builds decentralized infrastructure for the Ethereum ecosystem. Its primary goal is to become a decentralized prediction market that uses the crowd to make predictions.

Name: Marinade Staked SOL
Description: Marinade.finance is the very first liquid staking protocol built on the Solana blockchain. The protocol allows users to stake and lock their SOL and in return receive MSOL, which can be freely used in DeFi. The project is moreover supported by the Solana Foundation themselves.

Name: Ravencoin
Description: Ravencoin is a digital peer-to-peer (P2P) network and is a blockchain-based service providing use case-specific functionality. The system was built to handle the specific function of transferring assets from one party to another efficiently by determining the truth about who owns what asset.

Name: Kava
Description: KAVA is the governance token of the cross-chain DeFi platform Kava. It's stablecoin lending system (powered by USDX), built on the Cosmos blockchain, utilizes collateralized debt positions (CDPs) to guarantee loans are always adequately back stopped. Borrowers who fail to maintain collateral above a threshold will have their assets seized by the Kava liquidator and sold through the auction module.

Name: IOST
Description: The IOST network is an ultra-fast, decentralized and fully functioning ecosystem with its own nodes, wallets, and a new consensus protocol based on the concept of proof-of-believability. The high number of transactions per second, the scalability and security of the blockchain, and the privacy protection it offers provide online service providers with infinite possibilities to serve their customers.

Name: WOO Network
Description: Woo Network provides traders, exchanges, institutions, and DeFi platforms with easy, low-cost access to best-in-class liquidity and trading execution. Holding WOO tokens enables users in both CeFi and DeFi products in the network to reduce fees and staking to very low costs.

Name: Moonbeam
Description: The Moonbeam blockchain is an Ethereum-compatible smart contract platform on Polkadot. Moonbeam provides an easy way for Ethereum developers to build or deploy Solidity projects within a Substrate environment.
